resources.string: Ready. Widget and quick tile can run the touch module reset.
Shizuku is not running. Start Shizuku, then return here.
Shizuku is running. Grant permission once to enable the fix.

Some aftermarket Galaxy A54 display assemblies can make the Synaptics touch controller report false touches after impact or screen replacement. Android sees them as real kernel input events, so regular app-level cancellation does not help.

This app asks Shizuku for shell-level access and sends Samsung’s TSP module reset command: module_off_master, wait two seconds, then module_on_master. It does not erase data, change firmware, or require root.
Use this as an emergency workaround. If the ghost touch returns often, the durable repair is still an OEM display/digitizer assembly and a proper flex, grounding, and frame-pressure inspection.
Add the widget to run the same reset without opening the app.
Open the app and grant Shizuku permission.
